Woe to those who are appointed to feed God’s people, but do not care to do them good! Here is a word of comfort to the neglected sheep. Even though only a remnant of God's flock remains, he will find them and they will be brought back to their former homes. Christ is spoken of as a branch from David’s family. He is righteous himself, and through him all his people are made righteous. Christ will break Satan's usurped power. All the spiritual descendants of believing Abraham and praying Jacob will be protected and saved from the guilt and dominion of sin. In the days of Christ's rule in the soul, the soul dwells at ease. He is here spoken of as "the Lord our Righteousness." He is so our Righteousness that no creature could be. His obedience to death is the justifying righteousness of believers and their title to heavenly happiness. And their sanctification, as the source of all their personal obedience, is the effect of their union with him and of the supply of this Spirit. By this name every true believer will call him and call upon him. We have nothing to plead but this: Christ has died; rather, he has risen again, and we have taken him for our Lord. This righteousness which he has accomplished to satisfy law and justice becomes ours; it is a free gift given to us through the Spirit of God, who puts it upon us, clothes us with it, enables us to lay hold on it, and claim an interest in it. "The Lord our Righteousness" is a sweet name to a convicted sinner; to one who has felt the guilt of sin in his conscience, seen his need for that righteousness, and understood its worth. This great salvation is far more glorious than all former deliverances of his church. May our souls be gathered to him and be found in him.

The false prophets of Samaria had deceived the Israelites into idolatry; yet the Lord considered the false prophets of Jerusalem guilty of even greater wickedness, by which the people were emboldened in sin. These false teachers would be compelled to suffer the most bitter part of the Lord's indignation. They made themselves believe that there was no harm in sin and practiced accordingly, then they made others believe so. Those who are determined to continue in evil ways will justly be given up to strong delusions. But which of them had received any revelation from God or understood anything of his word? There was a time coming when they would reflect on their folly and unbelief with remorse. The teaching and example of the true prophets led people to repentance, faith, and righteousness. The false prophets led people to rest in forms and notions and to be quiet in their sins. Let us take heed that we do not follow unrighteousness.

People cannot hide from God's all-seeing eye. Will they never see what judgments they are preparing for themselves? Let them consider what a vast difference there is between these prophecies and those delivered by the true prophets of the Lord. Let them not call their foolish dreams Divine oracles. The promises of peace that these prophets make are no more comparable to God's promises than chaff to wheat. An unhumbled heart is like a rock; if it is not melted by the word of God as fire, it will be broken into pieces by it as a hammer. How can they be safe or at ease for long who have a God of almighty power against them? The word of God is not a smooth, lulling, deceitful message. And by its faithfulness it can certainly be distinguished from false doctrines.

Those are miserable indeed who are forsaken and forgotten by God; and people’s mocking of God's judgments will not prevent them. God had taken Israel to be a people near to him, but they will now be cast out of his presence. It is a mark of great and daring impiety for people to jest with the words of God. Every idle and profane word will add to the sinner’s burden on the day of judgment, when everlasting shame will be his portion.
